states.name: Alabama
states.regulatorName: Alabama Dept. of Revenue — Motor Vehicle Division
states.proTip: Make sure your license, bond, and insurance all match up correctly. State minimums don't always reflect what your actual operation — or your lenders — may require.

states.name: Florida

dealerTypes.note: $10k–$20k based on number of licensed locations

states.garageLiabilityNote: Plus $10,000 PIP required. Transporter plate holders: minimum liability increases to $100,000.
states.proTip: Watch for entity and address mismatches between Sunbiz, your DMV license, and your insurance — they must match exactly. If you carry transporter plates, your minimum liability requirement jumps to $100,000.

states.name: Louisiana
states.proTip: Louisiana has TWO separate commissions — the LMVC handles new franchise dealers and the LUMVC handles used dealers. Renewal dates also vary by district. Know which office handles your renewal before you file anything.

states.name: Mississippi
states.proTip: Multi-location dealers regularly assume one license covers every lot — it usually doesn't. Wholesalers can also run into compliance issues when their bond and license no longer reflect how they actually operate day to day.

states.name: Tennessee
states.proTip: Tennessee renews on a biennial (every 2 years) cycle — not annual. Shared office or lot setups can create compliance issues if they don't meet commission facility standards, which are enforced during inspection and renewal.
